classData.comment:
/**
 * Conceptual-guide directories (non-recursive — only top-level `*.md`). Subdirectories
 * (`decisions/`, `tooling/`, `process/`) are reference/ADR substrate, intentionally excluded.
 */
classData.description: Conceptual-guide directories (non-recursive — only top-level *.md). Subdirectories (decisions/, tooling/, process/) are reference/ADR substrate, intentionally excluded.
classData.name: GUIDE_DIRS

classData.comment:
/**
 * Mermaid keywords that break the parser when reused as a node ID or `classDef` name.
 * `graph`/`flowchart` are also legal as the diagram-type declaration on the FIRST block line,
 * so the reserved-word node scan skips that declaration line (see {@link checkMermaidBlock}).
 */
classData.description: Mermaid keywords that break the parser when reused as a node ID or classDef name. graph/flowchart are also legal as the diagram-type declaration on the FIRST block line, so the reserved-word node scan skips that declaration line (see {@link checkMermaidBlock}).
classData.name: MERMAID_RESERVED

classData.comment: /** Above this node count an `LR` (left-to-right) flow squishes unreadable on GitHub/portal. */
classData.description: Above this node count an LR (left-to-right) flow squishes unreadable on GitHub/portal.
classData.name: LR_NODE_WARN

classData.comment:
/**
 * Headings that signal a reference catalog leaking into an explanation guide (Diátaxis).
 * Matched case-insensitively as a whole `##`/`###` heading line.
 */
classData.description: Headings that signal a reference catalog leaking into an explanation guide (Diátaxis). Matched case-insensitively as a whole ##/### heading line.
classData.name: FEATURE_LIST_HEADING

classData.comment:
/**
 * Tool-table parity (the OpenAPI-operation analogue of the dead-`ai:*`-script check). A guide that
 * inlines an MCP tool table must reference real `ai/mcp/server/*​/openapi.yaml` operationIds.
 *
 * **Why heading-scoped, not every `| `name` |` row:** config / property / schema tables share the
 * exact same row shape, so an unscoped scan flags `id`/`name`/`tier`/`schema` etc. as "hallucinated
 * tools" — empirically 56 false HARDs across the live guide corpus. We only scan rows under a
 * *tools-catalog* heading. The heading match is deliberately narrow (`(available|mcp|agent|the)? tools?`
 * anchored at the heading start) so `## Build Tools` / `## Debugging Tools` cannot match either.
 */
classData.description:
Tool-table parity (the OpenAPI-operation analogue of the dead-ai:*-script check). A guide that inlines an MCP tool table must reference real ai/mcp/server/*​/openapi.yaml operationIds.

Why heading-scoped, not every | name | row: config / property / schema tables share the exact same row shape, so an unscoped scan flags id/name/tier/schema etc. as "hallucinated tools" — empirically 56 false HARDs across the live guide corpus. We only scan rows under a tools-catalog heading. The heading match is deliberately narrow ((available|mcp|agent|the)? tools? anchored at the heading start) so ## Build Tools / ## Debugging Tools cannot match either.
classData.name: TOOLS_HEADING

classData.description: Returns the 1-based line number of a character index within content.
classData.name: lineOf

classData.comment:
/**
 * Extracts fenced ` ```mermaid ` blocks. `startLine` is the 1-based file line of the block's
 * FIRST body line (the line just after the opening fence), so `startLine + bodyIndex` yields the
 * exact file line of a finding inside the block.
 * @param {string} content
 * @returns {Array<{body: string, startLine: number}>}
 */
classData.description: Extracts fenced ```mermaid blocks. startLine is the 1-based file line of the block's FIRST body line (the line just after the opening fence), so startLine + bodyIndex yields the exact file line of a finding inside the block.
classData.name: extractMermaidBlocks

classData.comment:
/**
 * HARD checks for one Mermaid block: reserved-word node IDs / classDefs and self-loop edges.
 * The first non-empty line is the diagram declaration (`flowchart TD`, `graph LR`) and is
 * exempt from the reserved-word-as-node scan so the legal type keyword isn't flagged.
 * @param {{body: string, startLine: number}} block
 * @returns {Array<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>}
 */
classData.description: HARD checks for one Mermaid block: reserved-word node IDs / classDefs and self-loop edges. The first non-empty line is the diagram declaration (flowchart TD, graph LR) and is exempt from the reserved-word-as-node scan so the legal type keyword isn't flagged.

names: Array.<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>

classData.name: checkMermaidBlock

classData.description: WARN check: an LR/RL flow with more than {@link LR_NODE_WARN} distinct nodes.

names: Array.<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>

classData.name: checkMermaidOrientation

classData.comment:
/**
 * HARD check: relative `*.md` links whose target file is absent on disk.
 * Only relative links (starting with `.`) are resolved; external URLs and bare anchors are skipped.
 * @param {string} content
 * @param {string} fileDir absolute directory of the guide being linted
 * @param {Function} [existsFn] path-exists predicate, injectable for testing (defaults to fs.existsSync)
 * @returns {Array<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>}
 */
classData.description: HARD check: relative *.md links whose target file is absent on disk. Only relative links (starting with .) are resolved; external URLs and bare anchors are skipped.

params.description: absolute directory of the guide being linted
params.name: fileDir

params.description: path-exists predicate, injectable for testing (defaults to fs.existsSync)
params.name: existsFn

names: Array.<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>

classData.name: checkDeadLinks

classData.comment:
/**
 * HARD check: `ai:*` script references that are not real `package.json` scripts (the
 * hallucinated-command class).
 * Conservative — only flags backtick-wrapped `` `ai:foo` `` or `npm run ai:foo` forms, so prose
 * mentioning a namespace casually isn't caught.
 * @param {string} content
 * @param {Set<string>} scriptKeys the set of defined `package.json` script names
 * @returns {Array<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>}
 */
classData.description: HARD check: ai:* script references that are not real package.json scripts (the hallucinated-command class). Conservative — only flags backtick-wrapped `ai:foo` or npm run ai:foo forms, so prose mentioning a namespace casually isn't caught.

names: Array.<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>

classData.name: checkDeadScriptRefs

classData.comment:
/**
 * HARD check: tool-table rows that reference an MCP tool no server exposes — the OpenAPI-operation
 * analogue of {@link checkDeadScriptRefs}. Scoped to rows under a {@link TOOLS_HEADING} so config /
 * property / schema tables (same `| `name` |` shape) never false-positive. Generalizes the
 * per-guide `GuideToolParity` spec into the shared lint. Fenced code is skipped so an example table
 * in a code block doesn't trip it.
 * @param {string} content
 * @param {Set<string>} operationIds union of every MCP server's openapi.yaml operationIds
 * @returns {Array<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>}
 */
classData.description: HARD check: tool-table rows that reference an MCP tool no server exposes — the OpenAPI-operation analogue of {@link checkDeadScriptRefs}. Scoped to rows under a {@link TOOLS_HEADING} so config / property / schema tables (same | name | shape) never false-positive. Generalizes the per-guide GuideToolParity spec into the shared lint. Fenced code is skipped so an example table in a code block doesn't trip it.

params.description: union of every MCP server's openapi.yaml operationIds
params.name: operationIds

names: Array.<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>

classData.name: checkOpenApiToolParity

classData.description: WARN checks: feature-list-skeleton headings + identity-guard framework hits, both scanned outside fenced code blocks so example code doesn't trip them.

names: Array.<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>

classData.name: checkProse

classData.comment:
/**
 * Lints one guide's content and returns all findings. Pure: no fs/process access beyond the
 * injectable `existsFn`, so it is unit-testable in isolation.
 * @param {string} content
 * @param {{filePath: string, fileDir: string, scriptKeys: Set, operationIds: Set, existsFn: Function}} ctx scriptKeys = package.json script names; operationIds = MCP openapi operationIds (defaults to empty → tool-parity is a no-op); existsFn is optional (defaults to fs.existsSync)
 * @returns {Array<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>}
 */
classData.description: Lints one guide's content and returns all findings. Pure: no fs/process access beyond the injectable existsFn, so it is unit-testable in isolation.

params.description: scriptKeys = package.json script names; operationIds = MCP openapi operationIds (defaults to empty → tool-parity is a no-op); existsFn is optional (defaults to fs.existsSync)
params.name: ctx

names: Array.<{severity: string, rule: string, line: number, detail: string}>

classData.name: lintGuide

classData.comment:
/**
 * Discovers the in-scope guide files (top-level `*.md` under each {@link GUIDE_DIRS} entry).
 * @returns {string[]} repo-relative file paths
 */
classData.description: Discovers the in-scope guide files (top-level *.md under each {@link GUIDE_DIRS} entry).
classData.name: discoverGuides

classData.description: Union of every MCP server's openapi.yaml operationIds — the canonical tool surface a guide's tool tables must resolve against. Regex-extracted (no YAML dependency); a missing servers dir yields an empty set, which makes {@link checkOpenApiToolParity} a no-op rather than a false alarm.
classData.name: loadOperationIds

classData.description: Parses --warn-as-error / --help plus optional explicit file paths (default: discover all).
classData.name: parseArgs

classData.description: CLI entry. Returns a numeric exit code (no process.exit) so tests can drive it directly.
classData.name: runLint
